raw_output_payload.reasoning:
We need tar command. Subdirectories of /testbed: /testbed/dir1 etc. We want archive /backup1.tar containing those subdirectories but without the prefix "testbed". Means paths inside archive should not start with testbed. So use -C /testbed and then include *? But need subdirectories only. Use tar -C /testbed -cf /backup1.tar .? That would include all contents including testbed? Actually -C changes to /testbed then archive . includes all. But we want subdirectories only, not the root? If we want all subdirectories of /testbed, not including files directly under /testbed? Use find? Simpler: tar -C /testbed -cf /backup1.tar --no-recursion? Hmm.

Assume we want all subdirectories and their contents. Use tar -C /testbed -cf /backup1.tar $(ls -d */) . That will include directories names without prefix. So command: tar -C /testbed -cf /backup1.tar $(find . -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d -printf "%P\n")? Actually find . -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d -printf "%P\n" gives names without leading ./ . Then tar -C /testbed -cf /backup1.tar $(...). Let's output that.
